dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4791 IS: To abolish the Anti-Weaponization Fund, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Schumer (for himself, Mr. Bennet, Mr. Booker, Mr. Coons, Mr. Durbin, Mr. Gallego, Mr. Heinrich, Mr. Hickenlooper, Ms. Hirono, Mr. Kaine, Mr. Kim, Mr. King, Mr. Luján, Mr. Merkley, Mr. Padilla, Ms. Rosen, Mr. Schatz, Mr. Van Hollen, Mr. Welch, Mrs. Murray, Mr. Blumenthal, Mrs. Shaheen, Mr. Schiff, Mr. Whitehouse, and Ms. Baldwin) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To abolish the Anti-Weaponization Fund, and for other purposes.

section.header: Settlement agreement in Trump v. Internal Revenue Service

subsection.text: The Anti-Weaponization Fund established by the Attorney General in connection with the disposition in Trump v. Internal Revenue Service, No. 1:26-cv-20609–KMW (S.D. Fla.), is abolished.

subsection.header: Nullification of order relating to release of certain claims
subsection.text: The order of the Attorney General issued on May 19, 2026, relating to the release of certain claims shall have no force or effect.
